mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 5.26% represents a restrictive monetary stance. Sustained elevated FEDFUNDS above 5.0% signals continued credit tightening that depresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced corporate profitability. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS and direction_20d exceeds 0.05 in magnitude with opposite sign.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power. When UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, forward equity returns decline as earnings expectations are revised downward due to weakening demand and margin compression.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months, or correlation between UNRATE changes and direction_60d is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 367,539 million dollars reflects consumer spending momentum. A sustained decline in RSAFS growth rate below 2% year-over-year signals weakening aggregate demand, leading to negative equity returns as forward earnings growth expectations are revised downward over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of months when RSAFS YoY growth falls below 2%, or Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS growth and direction_20d is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 113.14 indicates manufacturing capacity utilization. When INDPRO declines for two consecutive months, it signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk. This triggers negative equity returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice inflation and growth risks simultaneously.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances following two consecutive INDPRO declines, or the average 5-day return following such declines is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1,525 thousand units and payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 137,622 thousand jointly capture construction and labor demand. When both HOUST declines > 5% MoM and PAYEMS growth slows below 100k/month, it signals a demand shock in the construction-employment nexus, producing negative 20-day returns as forward GDP growth expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% MoM AND PAYEMS growth < 100k/month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,525k and PAYEMS at 137,622k as of February 2007
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal produces lower Rank IC than either component alone over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) at 203.906 combined with elevated FEDFUNDS at 5.26% creates a real rate environment that constrains equity valuations. When CPI growth accelerates above 3% YoY while FEDFUNDS remains above 5%, the Fed's restrictive stance persists, producing negative 60-day returns as terminal rate expectations remain elevated.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 203.906 and FEDFUNDS at 5.26% as of April 2007
regime_hint: stagflation risk or high-inflation tightening cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both CPI YoY > 3% and FEDFUNDS > 5%, or the interaction term adds no explanatory power beyond individual series (F-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 137,622 thousand reflects labor market strength. In a late-cycle expansion regime, when PAYEMS growth decelerates below 50k/month for two consecutive months, it signals imminent labor market weakness that precedes equity market downturns by 20 trading days as forward earnings revisions turn negative.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market deceleration
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances when PAYEMS growth falls below 50k/month for two consecutive months, or the lead-lag relationship shows no significant predictive power (Granger causality p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 11,513 billion chained 2017 dollars combined with housing starts (HOUST) at 1,525 thousand captures aggregate demand momentum. In a late-cycle regime, when HOUST declines below 1,400 thousand while GDP growth slows below 2% annualized, it signals demand exhaustion that produces negative 60-day returns as recession risk repricing occurs.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11,513B and HOUST at 1,525k as of Q4 2006 and February 2007
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with demand deceleration and recession risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both HOUST < 1,400k and GDP growth < 2%, or the interaction signal shows lower predictive power than a simple GDP growth threshold alone (Rank IC comparison).
